rationale: The ad56c295 delta is type-safe, deterministic, and closes my round-1 approve's only carried-over gap. Both strconv.Quote call sites are correct: I independently probed both refusal paths (a hostile panel.gates key and a hostile substitutes self-map key, each carrying real BEL/ESC/embedded-newline bytes via YAML double-quoted escapes) with a standalone program run against the built package at ad56c295, and confirmed the message clause (%q) and recovery clause (now strconv.Quote) render the SAME escaped form in both places -- no raw control byte, no forged extra physical line. I verified the new test's fixture decodes as claimed: printing the raw file bytes and the resulting error text confirms YAML's \a/\e/\n double-quoted escapes produce actual 0x07/0x1b/0x0a bytes in the decoded map key, which strconv.Quote/%q then render safely as \a, \x1b, and \n respectively. I audited every other fmt.Errorf in config.go for the same double-embed class the fix targeted: all remaining new-in-this-bead sites either embed a config-controlled string exactly once via %q (never repeated raw in the recovery clause) or reference closed-enum values (gate names, static labels) that carry no attacker-controlled bytes -- the fix's claim that the other 6 messages are already safe holds. The cursorReset fixture correctly exercises both round-1 findings folded into one entry: the middle reviewer sets both Family ("legacy-family-should-lose") and Model ("claude-opus-4-8") with an explicit Lens, and the test asserts both that R2.Model wins (my round-1 gap) and that the cursor does not reset across the explicitly-lensed slot (F1's finding), producing R3=empirical-prober rather than the wrong-impl's author-of-record. Ran go build ./..., go vet on both touched packages, and the full internal/config + cmd/mindspec suites (including the two new/changed tests) after cleaning up my own scratch probes -- all green, working tree clean.
findings: Non-blocking, out of scope for this bead: internal/config/config.go's pre-existing (spec-109-era, untouched by either 68fe576e or ad56c295) loop.gate_authority validation at ~line 500 embeds the map key `k` via raw %s twice -- once in the message and once in the recovery clause -- the same double-embed class G1 found and this fix closed for the two spec-112 sites. It predates this bead and is outside the consolidated round-1 fix's stated scope ("the OTHER NEW refusal messages FROM THIS BEAD"), so it should not block round 2, but it's worth a follow-up hardening pass on the 109 code path.
Disposition of my own round-1 ask (Model-wins-over-Family untested branch): ADDRESSED -- folded into the cursorReset fixture as intended, verified passing.
